# Regenerating the map data

All the local map data lives in [`public/data/`](../public/data) and is loaded
at runtime using the base-aware URL pattern (so it works on GitHub Pages under
`/SpoonsHideAndSeek/`). Some of it is **hand-made source data**; the rest is
**generated by scripts**. This doc covers the generator scripts.

## What's generated vs. what's hand-made

**Hand-made source data** (created by you, e.g. exported from Google My Maps —
_not_ produced by any script, so don't expect a command to rebuild them):

| File                                     | What it is                                                            |
| ---------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `public/data/game-boundary.geojson`      | The play-area boundary polygon. Input to the POI script + app.        |
| `public/data/stops.geojson`              | Valid public-transport stops. Input to the hiding-zones script + app. |
| `public/data/google-map-export*.geojson` | Raw exports kept for reference.                                       |

**Generated by scripts** (safe to delete and rebuild):

| File(s)                                                         | Command                         |
| --------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------- |
| `hiding-zone-circles.geojson`, `hiding-zone-no-overlap.geojson` | `pnpm generate:spoons-zones`    |
| `pois/*.geojson`                                                | `pnpm generate:spoons-pois`     |
| `admin-councils.geojson`, `admin-districts.geojson`             | `pnpm generate:admin-districts` |

Prerequisite for all of them: `pnpm install` (the scripts use `@turf/turf` and
`osmtogeojson` from the project's deps). Run every command from the repo root.

---

## `pnpm generate:spoons-zones`

Script: [`scripts/generate-spoons-hiding-zones.mjs`](../scripts/generate-spoons-hiding-zones.mjs)

- **Input:** `public/data/stops.geojson`
- **Output:** `public/data/hiding-zone-circles.geojson` (a 500 m circle around
  every stop) and `public/data/hiding-zone-no-overlap.geojson` (those circles
  merged into one polygon).
- **Network:** none — pure Turf computation, runs in a second.
- **Re-run when:** you change `stops.geojson`, or want to change the 500 m
  radius / circle smoothness (`turf.circle([lng, lat], 500, { steps: 32, … })`
  in the script).

```bash
pnpm generate:spoons-zones
```

---

## `pnpm generate:spoons-pois`

Script: [`scripts/generate-spoons-pois.mjs`](../scripts/generate-spoons-pois.mjs)

Generates the point datasets behind the category questions (Greggs, cinemas,
museums, parks, …) — see [questions.md](./questions.md#category--poi-backed-questions-and-their-data).

- **Input:** `public/data/game-boundary.geojson` (for clipping).
- **Output:** one file per category in `public/data/pois/`, e.g.
  `theme_park.geojson` (Greggs), `cinema.geojson`, `park.geojson`.
- **Source:** one combined **Overpass** (OpenStreetMap) query for all
  categories, clipped to the boundary **+ a 16-mile buffer** (so tentacle
  questions can reach POIs just outside the play area). Address-less Greggs are
  then named via **Nominatim** reverse-geocoding.
- **Network:** yes, and it can be **slow** (minutes). The public Overpass
  mirrors rate-limit aggressively — the script cycles through several mirrors
  with backoff, so 429/406 messages in the log are normal; let it retry.
  Nominatim is polled at ~1 request/1.2 s, so lots of unnamed Greggs = longer.

```bash
pnpm generate:spoons-pois
```

### Customising it

- **Add/change a category:** edit `POI_SELECTORS` (top of the script) — each
  entry is `{ location, key, value }` where `key=value` is the OSM tag matched.
- **Greggs:** the `theme_park` slot is repurposed to `brand=Greggs`; naming
  logic (street suffix, reverse-geocode fallback) is in `buildName` /
  `reverseGeocodeStreet`.
- **Stores missing from OSM:** add them by hand to the `MANUAL_POIS` list — they
  are merged in after the Overpass fetch and **survive re-runs**. (This is how
  the Villette Road Greggs, absent from OSM, is included.)
- **Buffer size:** `BUFFER_MILES` (default 16).

> Because it re-queries live OSM, a re-run can shift names slightly (Nominatim
> is non-deterministic) or pick up new/removed POIs. Review the diff before
> committing.

---

## `pnpm generate:admin-districts`

Script: [`scripts/generate-admin-districts.mjs`](../scripts/generate-admin-districts.mjs)

Generates the boundaries for the **Administration District** matching question.

- **Output:** `public/data/admin-councils.geojson` (the 5 Tyne & Wear
  metropolitan boroughs) and `public/data/admin-districts.geojson` (their
  electoral wards).
- **Source:** the **ONS Open Geography Portal** (ArcGIS), **December 2024**
  vintage, BGC (generalised & coastline-clipped), filtered to the 5 Tyne & Wear
  LAD codes listed in `TYNE_AND_WEAR_LADS`.
- **Network:** yes, but ArcGIS is reliable and fast (a few seconds) — no
  rate-limit dance like Overpass.

```bash
pnpm generate:admin-districts
```

### Customising it

- **Newer ward vintage:** bump the `service` names (e.g. to a future
  `Wards_December_2025_Boundaries_UK_BGC` / matching LAD service). Find current
  service names in the ONS ArcGIS services list.
- **Different area:** change `TYNE_AND_WEAR_LADS` to the LAD codes you want.

> Why ONS and not OSM: OSM has the councils (`admin_level=8`) but England's
> urban areas are largely unparished, so it has no electoral-ward boundaries for
> Tyne & Wear. ONS provides both, so both come from there.

---

## Full refresh

If you've changed the source data or just want everything rebuilt:

```bash
pnpm install                     # once
pnpm generate:spoons-zones       # needs stops.geojson
pnpm generate:spoons-pois        # slow; needs game-boundary.geojson + network
pnpm generate:admin-districts    # fast; network
```

Then sanity-check and commit the changed files under `public/data/`. A quick
validity check for any output:

```bash
node -e "const fc=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('public/data/pois/theme_park.geojson','utf8')); console.log(fc.features.length,'features')"
```

Finally, `pnpm build` (or `pnpm dev`) and confirm the map still loads and the
affected questions work.
